objective: Create a real loopback-only Ollama/OpenAI-compatible text model gateway with provider/model health, streamed tokens, bounded timeout and retry, circuit breaking, live NVIDIA telemetry, and mandatory resource leases that retain at least 2048 MiB VRAM headroom.
non_goals: Do not download, promote or claim quality for a model automatically.
Do not return canned or fake assistant responses when the configured provider/model is unavailable.
Do not send prompts, API keys or model responses to logs, audit records or non-loopback endpoints.
Do not implement long-term memory, speech, avatar, perception or tool execution.
contracts: Provider base URL must resolve to a numeric or named loopback host and credentials are never returned by status APIs.
Every GPU-backed model stream must hold an active resource lease before network inference begins and release it on success, error or cancellation.
Live NVIDIA telemetry admission retains at least 2048 MiB free VRAM; missing telemetry fails closed for GPU inference.
The gateway emits only real provider tokens and returns an explicit unavailable/error state when no provider or model is reachable.
Retry applies only before any response token is emitted; partial streams are never silently replayed.
Repeated provider failure opens a bounded circuit and recovery uses a half-open probe.
allowed_dependencies: Python 3.12+ standard library and existing Hina runtime modules.
acceptance_criteria: A local test HTTP server proves Ollama and OpenAI-compatible health and token streaming adapters.
Provider URLs outside loopback, malformed stream payloads, timeout and unavailable models fail explicitly without fake text.
Resource tests cover live telemetry admission, 2048 MiB headroom, lease release, timeout and higher-priority preemption callback.
Gateway tests cover retry-before-first-token, no retry after a partial stream, circuit open and recovery.
Runtime exposes real provider/resource status while remaining startable when Ollama is absent.
